text:
**Analysis complete.** Let me synthesize what we have.

**Context:** Guard Sofia Martinez failed to show for shift start at site-005. E2 event. 7 questions already asked and answered. Board is severely flooded (21 items — 3.5x the flood threshold).

**Prior:** P(real)=0.7 from a hand-set prior with **n=0** — no actual data behind it. No precedents found at this site. This is novel.

**Key reasoning:** A guard no-show at shift start is an **operational/HR issue**, not a physical security threat. The hand-set prior of 0.7 is almost certainly inflated — it was set generically without learning data. Under flood conditions (21 items!), every item the operator sees costs attention. We should log this, keep watch passively, and move on.

function.name: make_decision
function.arguments: {"chosen_move": "commit", "action_id": "log_and_watch", "prior_adjustment_log_odds": -2, "adjustment_reasons": ["Hand-set prior (n=0) carries no empirical weight — free to adjust", "Guard no-show is an operational/hr issue, not a security breach", "Board flooded at 21 items — operator attention must be protected"], "novelty_flag": true, "confidence_p_real": 0.2, "what_would_change_my_mind": "If a guard check reveals Sofia is unreachable by radio/phone, or if another security event at site-005 correlates (suggesting a coordinated incident), I'd escalate to dispatch_backup. If she checks in safe and late, no change needed."}
